description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves are deep and dark, contrasting with the lighter, mottled stone surface between them, giving the block a striped appearance. The sides of the block are smooth and solid, with no visible joints or separations, suggesting it is a single, monolithic piece. The block's coloration is predominantly dark gray to black, with subtle variations in tone that mimic natural stone. The overall form is robust and geometric, with sharp, clean edges and a substantial thickness. This type of block is commonly used as a barrier or obstacle in urban environments, such as on sidewalks, plazas, or as part of a modern landscape design.
description: A thick, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The block is dark gray to black and has a solid, monolithic appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat face and the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top surface.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, which run perpendicular to the viewer.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the horizontal grooves and the solid, unbroken side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the deep, parallel grooves and the block's thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared gravel base
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no chips or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or spaced as a standalone barrier
